title: Web Basics: HTTP Methods — GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
content:
### Day 166: Web Basics: HTTP Methods — GET, POST, PUT, DELETE

**HTTP Methods:**
- **GET:** Retrieve data (idempotent)
- **POST:** Create new resource
- **PUT:** Update entire resource
- **DELETE:** Remove resource

**Status Codes:** 200 OK, 201 Created, 400 Bad Request, 404 Not Found, 500 Server Error
codeExample:
# Day 166: Web Basics: HTTP Methods — GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
# HTTP Methods explained
import json

# Simulating API requests
endpoints = {
    "GET /users": {"method": "GET", "desc": "List all users", "status": 200},
    "POST /users": {"method": "POST", "desc": "Create user", "status": 201},
    "GET /users/1": {"method": "GET", "desc": "Get user by ID", "status": 200},
    "PUT /users/1": {"method": "PUT", "desc": "Update user", "status": 200},
    "DELETE /users/1": {"method": "DELETE", "desc": "Delete user", "status": 204}
}

print("REST API Design:")
for endpoint, info in endpoints.items():
    print(f"  {info['method']:6s} {endpoint:15s} → {info['status']} {info['desc']}")
